name: Circular Route 32 of Serra do Açor
name_original: Percurso circular 32 da Serra do Açor
region_intro: Touring cycling around Coja offers routes through a landscape characterized by varied elevations and river valleys. The region features a mix of forested areas and open terrain, providing diverse cycling experiences. Many routes navigate the foothills of the Serra do Açor, presenting both moderate and challenging climbs. This area is suitable for touring cyclists seeking routes with significant elevation changes and scenic views. Best touring cycling routes around Coja The most popular touring cycling route is Circular Route 32 of Serra do Açor, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Serra do Açor region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fonte de Amandos – Nogueira Village loop from Arganil, a difficult 30.6 miles (49.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Fonte de Amandos – Passadiços do Cerro da Candos loop from Arganil, a 39.0 miles (62.8 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Coja is defined by hilly terrain, river valleys, and forested areas. The network offers options for moderate to challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Coja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Coja?
A: There are currently 9 touring cycling routes available around Coja on komoot, offering diverse experiences through hilly terrain and river valleys. These routes cater to moderate to difficult ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Coja?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling in Coja, with milder temperatures and less rainfall. Summers can be quite warm, especially in the interior, while winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Coja?
A: The touring cycling routes around Coja are primarily categorized as moderate to difficult due to the region's hilly terrain and significant elevation changes. While there are no easy routes listed, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292075686/circular-route-32-of-serra-do-acor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route 32 of Serra do Açor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that might be suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Which routes offer a challenging touring cycling experience?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Coja offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32159557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Amandos – Nogueira Village loop from Arganil&lt;/a&gt; is a 30.6-mile path with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32191648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Amandos – Passadiços do Cerro da Candos loop from Arganil&lt;/a&gt;, covering 39.0 miles through hilly landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Coja area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Coja are designed as loops. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2292075686/circular-route-32-of-serra-do-acor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Route 32 of Serra do Açor&lt;/a&gt;, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32168726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Amandos – Côja loop from Carapinha&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32165039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Côja loop from Tábua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes around Coja?
A: Touring cycling routes around Coja are characterized by varied elevations, river valleys, and a mix of forested areas and open terrain. You can expect scenic views of the Serra do Açor foothills and the surrounding natural landscapes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can be found near the cycling routes?
A: The Coja area offers several points of interest. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7272114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Sumes&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraga da Pena Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5843352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colorful Boardwalks of the Trilho dos Gaios&lt;/a&gt;. There are also viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/400928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic View of Serra da Lousã&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options in Coja?
A: Yes, Coja features several longer touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32191648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Amandos – Passadiços do Cerro da Candos loop from Arganil&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest at 39.0 miles (62.8 km), offering an extensive ride through the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Coja?
A: The touring cycling routes around Coja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's varied terrain, the challenge of the climbs, and the scenic beauty of the Serra do Açor foothills and river valleys.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or pubs near the touring cycling trails in Coja?
A: While specific amenities vary by route, Coja and nearby villages offer local cafes and restaurants where cyclists can refuel. It's advisable to check the route details or local maps for establishments along your chosen path, especially in smaller, more remote areas.

Q: What are the typical terrain conditions for touring cycling in Coja?
A: The terrain around Coja is predominantly hilly, with routes navigating river valleys and the foothills of the Serra do Açor. Cyclists should expect a mix of paved roads and potentially some gravel sections, with significant elevation changes throughout the region.
tips: This section follows the Alva River Valley, between agricultural fields and forests, until reaching the stone bridge in Barril de Alva. The trail is dirt and requires some caution, but the serenity of the landscape and the murmur of the river make every pedal stroke worthwhile.
